variants.hypothesis: Removing the talent objection will increase first-three-second retention.
variants.hook: Your first sketch is allowed to be bad.
variants.payoff: Ten imperfect minutes gives you a place worth remembering.

scenes.label: hook
scenes.narration: Your first sketch is allowed to be bad.
scenes.visual: A blank pocket sketchbook opens on a cafe table.

scenes.label: practice
scenes.narration: Choose one doorway, draw only the largest shapes, and stop after ten minutes.
scenes.visual: Three bold shapes appear over a loose doorway sketch.

scenes.label: payoff
scenes.narration: Ten imperfect minutes gives you a place worth remembering.
scenes.visual: The finished page sits beside the real doorway.

variants.visualDirection: Warm documentary phone footage, real ink lines, no synthetic artwork.
variants.caption: A ten-minute urban sketch is a memory practice, not a talent test.
variants.cta: Use the field-note prompt in the full article.

provenance.audience: Curious adults starting a creative hobby

variants.hypothesis: A bounded ten-minute challenge will increase saves and completions.
variants.hook: Draw this doorway before your coffee cools.
variants.payoff: You leave with a page that records where you were.

scenes.label: hook
scenes.narration: Draw this doorway before your coffee cools.
scenes.visual: Coffee steam rises beside a doorway and pocket sketchbook.

scenes.label: challenge
scenes.narration: Block the frame, add three shadows, then write the street and date.
scenes.visual: A hand draws the frame, shadows, street, and date in four quick cuts.

scenes.label: payoff
scenes.narration: You leave with a page that records where you were.
scenes.visual: The sketchbook closes as the person leaves the cafe.

variants.visualDirection: Fast tactile close-ups of real paper, ink, coffee, and street details.
variants.caption: Try one doorway in ten minutes. The goal is a field note, not a portfolio piece.
variants.cta: Open the full field-note guide and choose your first view.

provenance.audience: Busy beginners who want a bounded creative practice
